chore(beads): flush issue tracker updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -365,7 +365,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.8","title":"Improve sequence diagram fragment rendering (loop/alt/par nesting)","description":"Reality check found: Sequence diagrams are marked 'Partial' in the README. The parser comprehensively captures fragments (loop, alt, par, opt, neg), boxes, notes, activations, and 26 arrow types. But layout/rendering of fragment nesting may be incomplete.

## Scope
1. Create comprehensive test inputs with nested fragments:
   a. Simple fragment: 'loop Every minute; A->>B: ping; end'
   b. Nested alt/else: 'alt success; A->>B: ok; else failure; A->>B: err; end'
   c. Double-nested: 'loop; alt; A->>B: yes; else; A->>B: no; end; end'
   d. Par blocks: 'par; A->>B: one; and; A->>C: two; end'
   e. Fragment with box: 'box Group; participant A; participant B; end'
2. Render each to SVG and terminal, inspect output:
   a. Are fragment boundaries visually rendered (labeled rectangles)?
   b. Are nested fragments properly indented/contained?
   c. Do fragment labels (loop, alt, else, par) appear in the output?
   d. Are activation bars rendered on lifelines?
3. If fragments are NOT visually rendered:
   a. Add fragment boundary rendering to fm-render-svg: draw labeled rounded-rect for each fragment region
   b. Add fragment support to fm-render-term: draw box chars around fragment regions
   c. The LayoutExtensions.sequence_fragments (or similar) field should carry fragment geometry
4. If fragments ARE rendered but poorly:
   a. Fix nesting overlap issues
   b. Ensure label positioning doesn't collide with messages
5. Once complete: update README status from 'Partial' to 'Implemented'

## Test Plan
- Golden SVG test for each of the 5 fragment test cases above
- Assert SVG output contains 'rect' elements for fragment boundaries (not just messages)
- Assert terminal output has box-drawing characters around fragment regions
- Property test: any valid sequence input with fragments produces SVG with fragment-count rect elements

## Cross-reference
- bd-b0pc.11 (e2e smoke tests) depends on this
- No existing bead covers sequence fragment rendering specifically","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:32:34.341943887Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:32:34.341560741Z","close_reason":"Investigation found the full pipeline already works: parser captures fragments, layout computes geometry (build_sequence_fragment_geometry), SVG renders dashed labeled rects, terminal renders box chars. Added 6 end-to-end tests confirming loop/alt/par/nested fragments render correctly. Activation bars, notes, lifecycle markers, and mirror headers also fully implemented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","reality-check","render","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.8","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.665916008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b0pc.9","title":"Add PNG rasterization smoke test under --features png","description":"Reality check found: README documents PNG output via '--features png' using resvg/usvg, but there are zero tests for this feature in the CI suite.

## Scope
1. Verify the png feature compiles: cargo check -p fm-cli --features png
2. If it fails: fix Cargo.toml dependency versions for resvg/usvg, update any API changes
3. Create integration test in fm-cli/tests/ (feature-gated with #[cfg(feature = "png")]):
   a. Render 'flowchart LR; A-->B-->C' to PNG via the render pipeline
   b. Verify output file exists and is non-empty
   c. Check PNG magic bytes: first 8 bytes should be [0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A]
   d. Verify image dimensions are reasonable (> 100x100 pixels, < 10000x10000)
   e. Test with different themes: default and dark should produce different PNG content
4. Add CI job or test configuration that runs with --features png
5. If resvg/usvg are too heavy for CI, add a note about optional PNG support

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p fm-cli --features png -- png (run only PNG-specific tests)
- Assert PNG magic bytes
- Assert file size > 1KB (not empty/trivial)
- Assert multiple diagram types produce valid PNGs (flowchart + sequence at minimum)
- Log file sizes and generation times for performance baseline

## Cross-reference
- PNG feature is documented in README under 'fm-cli render' and under 'Feature Flags'
- No existing bead covers PNG testing","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:41:50.342685190Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T04:41:50.342414718Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["png","reality-check","render","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b0pc.9","depends_on_id":"bd-b0pc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:38:40.852743651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b2l4","title":"Harden SVG link emission against unsafe hrefs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T13:32:26.069772029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:38:26.609990321Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:38:26.609716050Z","close_reason":"Hardened SVG link emission against unsafe hrefs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-b3h0","title":"Prepare crates.io publishing strategy for workspace crates","description":"## Problem
README admits: "frankenmermaid is not published on crates.io yet, so cargo install frankenmermaid is not a working install path today." This is honest but represents a product gap. Users who expect cargo install will be disappointed.

## Challenges
The workspace has 8 crates (fm-core, fm-parser, fm-layout, fm-render-svg, fm-render-term, fm-render-canvas, fm-wasm, fm-cli). Publishing to crates.io requires:
1. Each crate must have unique names (fm-core might conflict; may need frankenmermaid-core etc.)
2. Dependencies between crates must use crates.io versions, not path dependencies
3. The FNX git dependency (franken_networkx) must be optional and not required for the default publish
4. License, description, documentation fields must be filled in every Cargo.toml
5. Publishing order must respect dependency graph: fm-core first, then fm-parser, etc.

## Scope
1. Audit each crate's Cargo.toml for crates.io readiness (name uniqueness, required fields, dependency types).
2. Choose crate names (e.g., frankenmermaid-core, frankenmermaid-parser, etc. — or use fm- prefix as-is if available).
3. Check name availability on crates.io for all 8 crate names.
4. Add missing fields (description, documentation, homepage, repository, keywords, categories) to each Cargo.toml.
5. Create a publishing script or document the manual publish order.
6. Test with `cargo publish --dry-run` for each crate.

## Dependencies
- FNX integration must be cleanly optional (it already is via feature flags, but verify no compile failures with default features on a clean checkout).

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo publish --dry-run` succeeds for all 8 crates.
- Publishing order documented.
- Names reserved on crates.io (or verified available).
- README updated with `cargo install frankenmermaid` (or whatever the final binary crate name is).","notes":"Blocker: franken-kernel is a git dependency used by fm-core. Either publish franken-kernel to crates.io first, or vendor its code. Also need to add metadata (description, repository, etc.) to all crate Cargo.tomls.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:23.518446553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-13T23:53:59.027739817Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b3h0","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.019576375Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b3h0","depends_on_id":"bd-w9id","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:39.601126552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-b65r","title":"Add comprehensive classDef/style test suite","description":"## Problem
classDef/style is getting wired (bd-65l0) but needs dedicated test coverage to prevent regression.

## Scope
1. **Parser tests**: Verify classDef definitions with various CSS properties are correctly stored in IR.
2. **Render tests**: Verify classDef produces correct SVG attributes/styles.
3. **Precedence tests**: theme defaults < classDef < style directive < init override.
4. **Edge cases**: empty classDef, classDef with quoted values, multiple classes on one node, classDef applied to nodes that don't exist.
5. **Golden test**: flowchart_with_styles.mmd exercising classDef + style + multiple classes.

## Dependencies
- bd-65l0 (classDef wiring) should be done first, but parser tests can be written independently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 10+ unit tests covering classDef parsing, rendering, and precedence.
- 1 golden test for styled flowchart.
- Regression-safe: any future change to classDef handling will break a test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:26:48.968254255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-14T05:07:34.620680056Z","closed_at":"2026-04-14T05:07:34.620411458Z","close_reason":"Added 5 new edge case tests for classDef: empty definition, quoted values, multiple classes, nonexistent node, inline declaration. Total: 11+ classDef tests + 1 golden test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-b65r","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:06.566949423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-b65r","depends_on_id":"bd-65l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:27:03.073111246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-b6gg","title":"Implement accDescription and accTitle accessibility directives","description":"# Accessibility Directives

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
accTitle: Process Flow
accDescr: This diagram shows the order processing pipeline from receipt to delivery.
accDescr {
  Multi-line description
  of the diagram's purpose
}
\`\`\`
- accTitle: sets the accessible title for screen readers (maps to SVG <title> element)
- accDescr: sets the accessible description (maps to SVG <desc> element)
- Both are global, apply to the entire diagram
- Used by all diagram types

## Current State
- accTitle/accDescr are NOT parsed
- SVG renderer already has accessibility support (a11y) but doesn't read these from IR

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Recognize accTitle and accDescr (single-line and multi-line block) directives. Store in MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **IR**: Add accessible_title: Option<String> and accessible_description: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Emit <title> and <desc> elements within the root <svg> element.
4. **Tests**: Single-line accTitle, accDescr, multi-line accDescr block, SVG output verification.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:12.842946048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.129238031Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T05:17:20.128753604Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["a11y","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:32:22Z"},{"id":194,"issue_id":"bd-b6gg","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. accTitle: 'Process Flow' → verify <title> element in SVG root
2. accDescr: 'Single line description' → verify <desc> element in SVG root
3. accDescr { multi-line } → verify multi-line <desc>
4. Both accTitle and accDescr → verify both elements present
5. Neither present → verify no empty <title>/<desc> elements
6. Works with flowchart, sequence, class, state → verify across diagram types","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bds1","title":"Sequence parity: dotted-cross distinction and central-offset nuance","description":"Follow-up parity bead for remaining sequence message semantics after half-arrow and central connection support. Scope: preserve dotted cross vs solid cross distinction end-to-end and verify whether central connection syntax needs additional visual offset or marker treatment beyond current lifeline-centered rendering. Add focused tests and evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:30:20.033573036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T18:56:03.454683759Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T18:56:03.454147313Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","sequence"]}
